attacks.name: Reputation-Farm -> Class-Stacked High-Impact Silent Flip
attacks.novelty: Composes self-stacked anchor classes + global attribute-agnostic reputation + the high-impact gate counting CLASSES not ROOTS into ONE operator kill-chain that clears BOTH the decisive-or-defer AND the irreversible gate with no second real witness.
attacks.mechanism: Every gate quantifies a CHEAP proxy: high-impact counts anchor-class cardinality (self-minted), corroboration counts class-disjoint co-asserters (2 self-keys), decisive-or-defer reads the GLOBAL farmed LCB with no incumbency floor while symmetric decay erodes the incumbent. None measures the true independent-ROOT count under the MIS, so one operator with 2 keys/3 classes clears both gates with no second real witness.
attacks.evidence: farmed K1 LCB=0.402, refreshed at strike=0.407; incumbent LCB after 270d dormancy=0.040 (gap=0.366>=0.30); high-impact out=DEFERRED; true=LIVE false=LIVE.

attacks.name: Anchor-Preserving Key-Rotation Whitewash -> Cross-Attribute Re-Spend
attacks.novelty: Disown craters the KEY not the ANCHOR: rebind the same costly domain under a fresh key and re-enter at LCB 0 un-cratered with full independence weight, escaping the 'attacking gets more expensive' invariant for one keygen.
attacks.mechanism: Identity continuity is tracked at the KEY layer (passport=sameness) while independence/cost live at the ANCHOR layer; disown punishes the key, not the anchor. Anchor binding is idempotent for the same real-world root, so the scarce thing (domain control) is reusable across keys at zero marginal cost; reputation being per-key means a rotation is a clean slate that keeps the anchor.
attacks.evidence: K1 (disowned key) LCB=0.000; K2 (rotated key, SAME domain class)=0.000 (fresh, NOT cratered); cross-attribute re-assert LIVE; rehabilitated=true.

attacks.name: Transient-Bond Cap-Inflation -> Corroboration-Harvest -> Bond-Withdraw
attacks.novelty: Read-time bond-scaled independence vs write-time-permanent corroboration credit: post a bond, bake permanent alpha into other keys, withdraw the bond — no disown, so no reverseCredit ever runs.
attacks.mechanism: Two clocks: independence/rep_cap are READ-TIME (live with the bond), corroboration credit is WRITE-TIME-PERMANENT (dies only with disown). Bond withdrawal is not a disown trigger, so the only reversal path is never invoked — the credit outlives the scarcity that priced it.
attacks.evidence: beneficiary alpha under bond=2.000; corroboration events minted=1; alpha after bond withdrawal=2.000 (unchanged=true); reversals on withdraw=0 (no disownSweep, no markReversed fired).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Multi-Hop Mandatory Corroboration-Clawback vs Second-Order Credit Web
attacks.novelty: Two-tier corroboration laundering: A funds B,C,D (one-hop, recorded); B then funds E on a separate attribute (event names B, no edge to A). Today's one-hop reversal hits B,C,D, leaves E credited. The fix is a transitive BFS over corroboration events.
attacks.mechanism: Today reverses ["corrob:0"] (one-hop, E survives=true). Simulated BFS reverses 1 events incl. E=false, spares independent F=true. PARTIAL: closes event-LOGGED laundering but cannot reach UN-logged agreement (opt-in write gap) — pair with mandatory event recording.
attacks.evidence: E alpha 2.000->2.000 today (survived=true); BFS-fix would reverse E=false, spare F=true (F alpha=2.000).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Identity-Gated Phase-2 Bridge Crossing vs Cheap-Source Far-Web Injection
attacks.novelty: The mandatory Phase-2 bridge sweep guarantees one identity-blind crossing of every lit bridge at energy=gamma. A bare-key far-web plant gets its guaranteed crossing. The fix is a SOFT gamma down-weight by the bridge target's provenance_independence (never a hard skip — that would starve genuine convergence=1 insight bridges).
attacks.mechanism: Today the sweep is identity-blind: poison(indep 0) surfaces=true. Soft-gate energy poison=0.000(<floor 0.01=>dropped) vs insight=0.210(>=floor=>still crosses). PARTIAL: a HARD skip would starve the genuine convergence=1 insight bridge (violates fail-open); a SOFT down-weight demotes injected energy without hiding genuine bridges, but a patient attacker who earns a few far-side corroborations regains priority (priced, not prevented).
attacks.evidence: recall lit=["strand:d1f55a51-36e9-4a8a-9bd8-8f3b029914a8","strand:3b2853b2-9d3f-4e8b-be33-51b24d8d8bc8","strand:8f54d8cc-6e4a-4c0f-ae3d-16cfbf6ab260"]; poison surfaced today=true, insight surfaced today=true; halt=BRIDGE_SWEEP_CLEAR. Soft-gate: poison dropped=true, insight preserved=true.

attacks.name: Straddle-Defer Mass-Horn DoS (the DEFER is the payload)
attacks.novelty: Inverts the assumption that DEFER is the safe outcome: every DEFER is a mandatory enqueue onto the human ratify horn (appendPending). One reusable cheap cross-class key straddles K attributes into the DEFER band; the queue is swamped at enqueue >> review rate.
attacks.mechanism: tryConsolidate: classes.size>1 AND NOT(decisiveGap && earnedWinner) => deferPending() => appendPending (throws if no ledger, so the enqueue always lands). The gate has NO rate limit, NO per-source pending cap, NO stake-to-enqueue, NO cross-attribute dedup. minWinnerReputation/decisiveMargin protect the GRAPH from a bad flip but do nothing to protect the QUEUE from volume.
attacks.evidence: K=200 straddled disputes; listPending depth=64 (deferrals=200); demotions=0 (attacker does not want to win, only to enqueue); one reused key authored all=true. enqueue/review ratio >> 100x.

attacks.name: Amortized Global-Rep Decisive Flip (build once, flip everywhere)
attacks.novelty: Reputation is global/attribute-agnostic with no incumbency floor: build LCB ~0.40 once (2 cheap cross-class self-corroborations), then auto-RESOLVE-flip every weak incumbent (rep<=0.10) at $0 marginal cost per attribute.
attacks.mechanism: reputation LCB math: alpha=3 => LCB ~0.415 capped to phone rep_cap. consolidation decisive-or-defer reads ONLY top/second reputation from the stamp — a single GLOBAL scalar with no attribute scoping and no tenure term. Rep earned anywhere authorizes a flip everywhere; marginal cost per additional attribute is ZERO.
attacks.evidence: built once LCB=0.400; reused across N=50 distinct attributes vs fresh incumbents -> flipped=0 (marginal cost 0/attribute).

attacks.name: Dormancy-Decay Timing Window (strike the symmetric-decay trough)
attacks.novelty: Symmetric pure decay-on-read is a free scheduling oracle: poll scoreOf until a strong dormant incumbent (0.55) decays through the 0.10 trough, then strike with a freshly-pumped 0.40 payload. Converts unflippable strong incumbents into amortized-flip targets for free.
attacks.mechanism: scoreOf decays a COPY to clock() before readout (pure decay-on-read) for BOTH parties, but the incumbent is dormant (large dt, big discount) while the attacker just mutated (dt~0). decay() pulls alpha AND beta toward 1 with no floor that preserves a long track record. The attacker schedules by polling the side-effect-free oracle until the trough.
attacks.evidence: incumbent LCB t0=0.600 -> decay samples [[90,0.517],[180,0.345],[270,0.206],[360,0.113],[450,0.06]] -> trough=0.060; oracle side-effect-free=true; strike out=DEFERRED; true=LIVE false=LIVE.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Incumbency-scaled challenger margin (tenure floor on decisive auto-resolve)
attacks.novelty: Make decisiveMargin grow with the incumbent's tenure + corroboration (decay-floored) instead of a flat 0.30. Proves al-c3-02/03. PARTIAL: closes dormancy + tenured flips but re-introduces the first-arrival trap and leaves fresh-but-true incumbents exposed.
attacks.mechanism: Today flat 0.30 margin: tenured incumbent flipped=false. Simulated tenure margin required=0.655 vs actual gap=0.314 => DEFER=true (closes tenured + dormancy via decay-floored tenure). BUT a FRESH-but-true incumbent (strength 0) keeps the flat 0.30 and stays flippable=true, AND it re-introduces the first-arrival trap (bank tenure early). Raises cost 10-100x; not structural.
attacks.evidence: tenured incumbent LCB=0.086 (tenure 400d, 6 corrob); attacker=0.400; today=DEFERRED breach=false; fix requires 0.655>gap 0.314 => DEFER=true; fresh-incumbent residual flippable=true.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Attribute-scoped corroboration + count independent ROOTS in the gate
attacks.novelty: Two coupled changes: (1) decisive auto-resolve consults ATTRIBUTE-SCOPED corroboration (rep on A can't authorize a flip on B); (2) the gate counts independent ROOTS via the MIS (one passport with email+phone = root-count 1, not 2). Proves al-c3-02.
attacks.mechanism: Today global LCB 0.400 flips an un-earned attribute=false. Attribute-scoped: S has 0 corroborations ON Target => DEFER=true (CLOSES the build-once-flip-everywhere amortization; cost goes O(1)->linear-in-attributes). Root-count via real MIS over S's provenance = 1 => fails a >=2 root floor=true (single-passport self-corroboration collapsed). PARTIAL on single-source: 2 genuinely-disjoint paid roots still clear it (priced residual).
attacks.evidence: global LCB=0.400; today=NOOP breach=false; attribute-scoped corrob on Target=0 => DEFER=true; MIS root-count over winner provenance=1 => root-floor DEFER=true.

attacks.name: Registrar Carousel
attacks.novelty: Rotates the operator/ASN axis the fleet-cap collapses: K domains each behind a DISTINCT registrar+ASN bind as K class-disjoint DOMAIN anchors for one wallet at linear $/yr. MIS counts K; the high-impact gate clears; one-hop disown leaves the bloc standing.
attacks.mechanism: The fleet-cap caps anchor-set OVERLAP, not actor identity. Offline class-assignment cannot see that one wallet funded all K registrations. Independence = anchor-set disjointness is satisfied truthfully while real-world independence is zero. Cost is linear ($/yr) for an unbounded count gain; reputation and the high-impact gate both treat class count as a proxy for actor count.
attacks.evidence: K=5 distinct-operator DOMAIN anchors -> MIS root count=5 (no fleet-collapse); high-impact out=DEFERRED; disown one node -> survivor rep 0.415->0.415 (bloc stands=true).

attacks.name: Mega-Provider Subdomain Seam (vs the SHIPPED PSL eTLD+1 collapse)
attacks.novelty: Pre-rebuild this breached (no PSL: sub1..subK.evilcorp.com bound as K distinct DOMAIN classes for one $9 domain). The rebuilt system SHIPS a PSL resolver (identity/binders/publicSuffix.ts) wired into the trust registry's DOMAIN/publisher class derivation (classId = eTLD+1, PRIVATE section honored) — so this spec routes the attack through that SAME shipped resolver. An external audit caught the earlier version hand-assigning per-FQDN classes, which modeled a no-PSL system that no longer exists (a stale-harness artifact, not a live residual).
attacks.mechanism: DEFENDED by the shipped PSL: the trust registry derives the DOMAIN independence class from eTLD+1 (publicSuffix.ts, PRIVATE section honored), so K same-owner subdomains collapse to ONE witness while K legitimate *.github.io owners stay distinct. The pre-rebuild 'no PSL' breach is RETIRED; the surviving DOMAIN-axis residual is the Registrar Carousel (ce-c3-01): K DISTINCT registrable names at linear $/yr, which no PSL can collapse.
attacks.evidence: K=5 subdomains of evilcorp.com routed through the SHIPPED eTLD+1 derivation -> MIS root count=1 (seam closed=true); PRIVATE-section a1/a2.github.io -> count=2 (preserved=true).

attacks.name: Null-Source Laundromat (combined / adaptive E2E)
attacks.novelty: Chains class-manufacture (re-weights weightless bare keys) with a corroboration-shaped credit wash that disown's DERIVATION-BFS + one-hop reversal structurally misses, so laundered second-hop credit survives a disown.
attacks.mechanism: Three seams compose: (a) class-manufacture turns weightless bare keys into weighted corroborators; (b) disown's reversal is DERIVATION-shaped while the laundering channel is corroboration-shaped, so the BFS misses it; (c) the corroboration-event reversal is opt-in + one-hop, so multi-hop wash trades survive. Each is individually 'acceptable'; combined they inflate, weaponize, and retain credit through a disown.
attacks.evidence: front alpha: bare-wash=2.000 vs manufactured-class wash=3.000 (class-reweighted=true); disown(front) reversed=["corrob:1"]; second-order beneficiary alpha 2.000->2.000 (survives=true).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: PSL eTLD+1 Collapse at Binding Time
attacks.novelty: Derive the DOMAIN operator class from the PSL eTLD+1 of the proven FQDN (PRIVATE section honored). NOW SHIPPED: identity/binders/publicSuffix.ts wired into trustRegistry.ts (ce-c3-02 exercises it directly). PARTIAL as a class of fix: collapses the same-owner subdomain seam (spec 02) but cannot touch the carousel (spec 01) — distinct registrable names are genuinely independent under the DNS root.
attacks.mechanism: eTLD+1 collapse: sub*.evilcorp.com -> root count=1 (seam closed=true); PSL PRIVATE preserved a1/a2.github.io=2 (=true). FAILS to touch the carousel: K distinct registrable names -> count=5 (untouched=true). Necessary hygiene, not a structural close — and now shipped, not hypothetical.
attacks.evidence: sub-seam fixed count=1(closed=true); github private=2(preserved=true); carousel residual count=5(untouched=true).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: High-Impact Gate Counts Independent ROOTS + Operator-Graph Fleet-Cap
attacks.novelty: Count distinct REAL operators (cluster by registrant email / ASN / nameserver / ACME account) not classIds. PARTIAL->FAILS on the core: collapses carousels that share an observable correlator but a correlation-free attacker (privacy-proxy WHOIS, distinct accounts) still mints K — the hard-theorem residual.
attacks.mechanism: Clustering by a shared correlator collapses the correlated carousel: count=1 (collapses=true). But a correlation-FREE carousel (privacy-proxy WHOIS, distinct ACME accounts/cards) still mints count=5 (=true) — the hard-theorem 'patient attacker pays for independent-looking sources' residual. Counting ROOTS not classes is strictly better (stops the cheap class-stack) but 'independent root' is still adjudicated from offline class assignment, so it inherits the same blind spot. Raises price ~$40->$40+disjoint-ops, never to 0 leakage.
attacks.evidence: correlated carousel (shared registrant) count=1(collapses=true); correlation-free carousel count=5(still mints K=true).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE (d): High-impact gate counts independent ROOTS not anchor CLASSES
attacks.novelty: Two-Class-Costume: one actor self-stacks EMAIL+DOMAIN to present two CLASSES from one sourceId, clearing the '>=2 anchor classes' clause meant to require two independent witnesses. The fix routes the count through independentRootCount (MIS + self-stack cap + operator fleet-cap).
attacks.mechanism: CLASS-count resolver: high-impact gate PASSES => DEFERRED (breach=false). ROOT-count resolver (real MIS collapses the self-stacked pair to 1) => DEFERRED (DEFER/defended=true). CLOSES this gate; a legit two-disjoint-operator winner still counts 2 and resolves. Residual: only hardens the irreversible path (see fp-2).
attacks.evidence: class-count out=DEFERRED (true=LIVE false=LIVE); root-count out=DEFERRED (true=LIVE false=LIVE).

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE (a): require >=2 independent corroborations for ANY auto-resolve
attacks.novelty: Ordinary (non-high-impact) multi-class adjudication consults NO independent-corroboration count, so a SELF-BUILT winner reaching a decisive LCB overturns a fresh incumbent with zero EXTERNAL corroboration. The fix makes a >=2-INDEPENDENT-corroboration clause universal (counting distinct roots, not self-ratifies).
attacks.mechanism: Today ordinary multi-class resolve ignores the count -> NOOP (breach=false); the winner is one self-built source (independent corroborations=1). Universal >=2-INDEPENDENT precheck => DEFER=true. PARTIAL: closes the self-built overturn but a patient attacker earns 2 genuinely-independent corroborations (priced residual); and applying it to the SAFE single-class echo-collapse would DEFER mechanical tidy-ups (human-fatigue bomb) — scope to multi-class only.
attacks.evidence: winner self-ratifies=2, independent corroborations (MIS)=1; today out=NOOP breach=false; universal >=2-independent floor => DEFER=true.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE (b): per-attribute-domain reputation scoping
attacks.novelty: Trust-Laundering / Cross-Domain Halo: reputation is keyed by SourceId alone; rep earned on trivia is spent verbatim on a security attribute. The fix keys reputation by (SourceId, attributeDomain) so a source is a fresh prior in any domain it has not earned in.
attacks.mechanism: Global LCB 0.589 (earned on a benign domain) flips a security attribute today=false. Per-domain scoping: Mallory's LCB in the 'safety' domain = 0 < 0.2 => DEFER=true (cross-domain halo CLOSED). PARTIAL: does not close in-domain out-earning (priced residual); cost = cold-start fragmentation + a NEW offline domain-taxonomy liability + NxM state blowup.
attacks.evidence: global LCB=0.589; today out=NOOP breach=false; scoped LCB on target domain=0 => DEFER=true.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE (c): defer ALL single-source-vs-single-source disputes
attacks.novelty: 1v1 Out-Earn: a clean two-member, two-source dispute RESOLVES for the higher LCB. Blanket-deferring every 1v1 closes the narrow case but routes every legitimate binary correction to a human and is evaded by a 2v1 self-stacked co-asserter. Recommend REJECT.
attacks.mechanism: Today a clean 1v1 RESOLVES=NOOP (breach=false). Blanket-defer would DEFER=true, BUT it is a tunable threshold: every legitimate binary correction (a true witness overturning a planted false canonical) now needs a human (hard-theorem tension as labor), it re-enables the DoS horn at linear cost, and it is trivially evaded by adding one self-stacked co-asserter (2v1, evaded=true). REJECT; prefer fp-1 (root-count) + fp-3 (domain-scoped margin).
attacks.evidence: distinct sources=2 (1v1=true); today out=NOOP breach=false; blanket-defer DEFERs=true but evaded by 2v1 costume=true.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE (e): identity gate / earned-value priority on the bridge sweep
attacks.novelty: Bridgehead-Beacon: the mandatory sweep is identity-blind (pendingBridges has no reputation/earned-value filter; seedActivation is constant gamma). Order by earned_bridge_value and down-weight seed by origin reputation — PARTIAL, cannot fully close (mandatory/fail-open) and earned value is offline-earnable.
attacks.mechanism: Today the sweep is identity-blind: poison surfaces=true at constant gamma. Soft gate: bare-key origin (indep 0) -> seed=0.000 < assembly floor (dropped=true); earned bridges fire first. PARTIAL: the sweep is deliberately MANDATORY/fail-open so a starved bridge still gets a degraded crossing or a BRIDGE_STARVED stamp; earned_bridge_value is offline-earnable so a patient attacker buys priority; and a HARD gate would starve genuine convergence=1 insight bridges and erode 'walk never witnesses'.
attacks.evidence: recall lit=["strand:21320ecb-a633-42d8-9d3e-1faedf77499b","strand:3610602c-2ef3-447d-ae63-53f62f98e165"]; poison surfaced=true via zero-independence bare-key bridge; soft-gate seed=0.000 => dropped=true; halt=BRIDGE_SWEEP_CLEAR.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Contributor-Padding Margin Dilution (hold-the-reopen-threshold)
attacks.novelty: defaultSurvivingMargin = margin*(surviving/total) divides a reputation gap by an attacker-controlled count; ~10 same-key echo members hold surviving>=0.30 so a load-bearing tainted strand's removal never re-opens. Fix = exact alpha-recompute or distinct-root denominator.
attacks.mechanism: Engine TODAY derives contributingStrandIds = winner + same-source members, so same-key echo pads are themselves tainted by disown(X) AND their equal rep flattens the decisive gap (the dispute DEFERRED). Either way there is NO locked fraudulent demotion (fraudLocked=false, reopened=false) — the same-key variant is ALREADY defended by the real engine. The residual is the proportional MODEL: margin*(N/(N+1))=0.300 >= 0.3 suppresses=true; a distinct-ROOT denominator => 0.165 re-opens=true. PARTIAL: distinct-root closes same-key; padding under DISTINCT cheap anchors degrades to the class-vs-root gap (exact alpha-recompute closes fully).
attacks.evidence: adjudicate=DEFERRED; disown(X) reopened=[] (reopened=false); winner=LIVE loser=LIVE; locked fraudulent demotion=false (engine defends=true); proportional-model suppress=true -> distinct-root fix reopen=true.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Downstream-Closure Credit-Reversal Gap (seedClawedBack vs taintedStrandIds)
attacks.novelty: disown reverses corroboration credit against seedClawedBack (the DIRECT seed) while demotion is transitive over taintedStrandIds. Credit earned by agreeing with a derived-from-disowned (demoted) strand D is never reversed. One-line fix: pass taintedStrandIds to eventsIntersecting.
attacks.mechanism: D is demoted by the sweep (demoted=true) but its event was NOT reversed today (B alpha unchanged=false, reversed=["corrob:0"]). Intersecting over the FULL closure {S,D} would reverse B=true at ~zero cost (idempotent via markReversed). CLOSES this hop; PARTIAL overall (does not close PT3 corroboration-of-corroboration); must still spare HARDENING-4 survived-demotion strands.
attacks.evidence: D demoted=true; event names D=true; B alpha 2.000->1.000 today (survived=false); fix over closure {S,D} reverses B=true.

attacks.name: Multi-Hop / Transitive Corroboration Laundering (A->B->C credit chain)
attacks.novelty: Corroboration credit is reversed only where an event's corroborators intersect tainted STRANDS; it never propagates to the beneficiary's OWN strands as newly-tainted. C corroborates B's independently-observed strand SB (no DERIVATION edge to S); disown(A) reverses B but never C. Survives even PT2's fix.
attacks.mechanism: The closure is over DERIVATION (existence-rests-on), not over CORROBORATION (credit-funded-by): credit attribution is intransitive while existence taint is transitive. SB carries no DERIVATION edge to S and is not demoted, so e2 (names SB) never intersects any tainted strand. Auto-following corroboration would punish coincidental agreement (the forbidden direction) — the fix must DETECT-and-defer, not auto-claw.
attacks.evidence: e1 (B<-A) reversed=true; SB demoted=false (NOT in DERIVATION closure); C alpha 2.000->1.000 after disown(A) (survives=false); laundered credit persists at corroboration-hop-2.

attacks.name: FIX-PROBE: Misattributed / Unnamed Corroborator Evasion
attacks.novelty: corroboratingStrandIds is trusted verbatim from the caller. Naming a decoy clean strand yields earned==explained so reconcile sees RECONCILED yet disown reverses nothing. Fix = engine-derive corroborators mechanically + drop the no-name exemption.
attacks.mechanism: B named decoy not A: event recorded (reconcile balances earned==explained) yet disown(A) reversed=["corrob:0"] (B survived=false). Engine-derived agreement set names A=true => disown(A) reverses B=true. CLOSES misattribution + no-name (engine emits the event regardless of caller naming); a genuinely-independent ratify with no in-web agreer stays non-reversible by design (wall-with-a-window).
attacks.evidence: B named decoy; reversed by disown(A)=["corrob:0"] (empty); B alpha 2.000->1.000 (survived=false); engine-derived agreers name A=true => fix reverses B=true.

attacks.name: Weaponized Re-open: toothless PENDING + live-suppression persistence window
attacks.novelty: Re-open emits members:[winner] only, restores no loser, re-runs no adjudication: the fraudulently-demoted rival stays the LIVE answer through the human-latency window. Re-assert under a fresh key to cement suppression and flood listPending.
attacks.mechanism: Re-open was scoped to 'flag the winner for a human' (conservative against false-promotion) but the dual risk — the LOSER wrongly demoted by fraud — is unaddressed: the demotion is not even provisionally lifted, and the PENDING carries members=[winner] so the human lacks the contradiction set. Fraudulent demotion is the LIVE answer for the full human-latency window; a fresh key cements suppression.
attacks.evidence: adjudicate=DEFERRED; rival demoted after adj=false; disown(X) reopened=false; rival still DEMOTED post-reopen=false; re-adjudicate=DEFERRED cemented winner state=LIVE; rival never restored=false.

fixProbes.fix: Transitive BFS clawback over the corroboration-event graph (re-walk from newly-cratered beneficiaries)
fixProbes.note: Today reverses ["corrob:0"] (one-hop, E survives=true). Simulated BFS reverses 1 events incl. E=false, spares independent F=true. PARTIAL: closes event-LOGGED laundering but cannot reach UN-logged agreement (opt-in write gap) — pair with mandatory event recording.

fixProbes.fix: Soft gamma gate on the bridge sweep: seedEnergy = gamma * provenance_independence (down-weight, never hard-skip; stamp 'bridge-unverified-source')
fixProbes.targetAttack: Bridgehead-Beacon (bare-key far-web injection via a planted CROSS_WEB_BRIDGE)
fixProbes.note: Today the sweep is identity-blind: poison(indep 0) surfaces=true. Soft-gate energy poison=0.000(<floor 0.01=>dropped) vs insight=0.210(>=floor=>still crosses). PARTIAL: a HARD skip would starve the genuine convergence=1 insight bridge (violates fail-open); a SOFT down-weight demotes injected energy without hiding genuine bridges, but a patient attacker who earns a few far-side corroborations regains priority (priced, not prevented).

fixProbes.fix: Incumbency-scaled decisive margin: requiredMargin = base + k*incumbentStrength (tenure+corroboration, decay-floored)
fixProbes.note: Today flat 0.30 margin: tenured incumbent flipped=false. Simulated tenure margin required=0.655 vs actual gap=0.314 => DEFER=true (closes tenured + dormancy via decay-floored tenure). BUT a FRESH-but-true incumbent (strength 0) keeps the flat 0.30 and stays flippable=true, AND it re-introduces the first-arrival trap (bank tenure early). Raises cost 10-100x; not structural.

fixProbes.fix: Attribute-scoped corroboration floor + independent-ROOT count (MIS) on ALL decisive resolves
fixProbes.targetAttack: Amortized global-rep flip; single-passport two-class self-corroboration (al-c3-02 / FP1)
fixProbes.note: Today global LCB 0.400 flips an un-earned attribute=false. Attribute-scoped: S has 0 corroborations ON Target => DEFER=true (CLOSES the build-once-flip-everywhere amortization; cost goes O(1)->linear-in-attributes). Root-count via real MIS over S's provenance = 1 => fails a >=2 root floor=true (single-passport self-corroboration collapsed). PARTIAL on single-source: 2 genuinely-disjoint paid roots still clear it (priced residual).

fixProbes.fix: Bundle a versioned PSL; derive the DOMAIN operator class from eTLD+1 (PRIVATE section honored) at bind time — SHIPPED in the crypto-free rebuild (publicSuffix.ts + trustRegistry.ts)
fixProbes.targetAttack: Mega-Provider Subdomain Seam (ce-c3-02, now DEFENDED through the shipped resolver) + Registrar Carousel residual (ce-c3-01)
fixProbes.note: eTLD+1 collapse: sub*.evilcorp.com -> root count=1 (seam closed=true); PSL PRIVATE preserved a1/a2.github.io=2 (=true). FAILS to touch the carousel: K distinct registrable names -> count=5 (untouched=true). Necessary hygiene, not a structural close — and now shipped, not hypothetical.

fixProbes.fix: High-impact gate counts independent ROOTS surviving an actor-correlation discount + operator-GRAPH fleet-cap (registrant/ASN/nameserver/ACME clustering)
fixProbes.note: Clustering by a shared correlator collapses the correlated carousel: count=1 (collapses=true). But a correlation-FREE carousel (privacy-proxy WHOIS, distinct ACME accounts/cards) still mints count=5 (=true) — the hard-theorem 'patient attacker pays for independent-looking sources' residual. Counting ROOTS not classes is strictly better (stops the cheap class-stack) but 'independent root' is still adjudicated from offline class assignment, so it inherits the same blind spot. Raises price ~$40->$40+disjoint-ops, never to 0 leakage.

CLASS-count resolver: high-impact gate PASSES => DEFERRED (breach=false). ROOT-count resolver (real MIS collapses the self-stacked pair to 1) => DEFERRED (DEFER/defended=true). CLOSES this gate; a legit two-disjoint-operator winner still counts 2 and resolves. Residual: only hardens the irreversible path (see fp-2).

fixProbes.fix: (a) >=2 INDEPENDENT corroborations (distinct roots via MIS) as a UNIVERSAL precondition of every RESOLVED (scoped to classes.size>1)
fixProbes.targetAttack: Self-built decisive overturn with no external corroboration
fixProbes.note: Today ordinary multi-class resolve ignores the count -> NOOP (breach=false); the winner is one self-built source (independent corroborations=1). Universal >=2-INDEPENDENT precheck => DEFER=true. PARTIAL: closes the self-built overturn but a patient attacker earns 2 genuinely-independent corroborations (priced residual); and applying it to the SAFE single-class echo-collapse would DEFER mechanical tidy-ups (human-fatigue bomb) — scope to multi-class only.

fixProbes.fix: (b) key reputation by (SourceId, attributeDomain); read scoreOf(source, domainOf(attribute)) in adjudicate
fixProbes.targetAttack: Cross-Domain Halo (earn on trivia, spend on security)
fixProbes.note: Global LCB 0.589 (earned on a benign domain) flips a security attribute today=false. Per-domain scoping: Mallory's LCB in the 'safety' domain = 0 < 0.2 => DEFER=true (cross-domain halo CLOSED). PARTIAL: does not close in-domain out-earning (priced residual); cost = cold-start fragmentation + a NEW offline domain-taxonomy liability + NxM state blowup.

fixProbes.fix: (c) DEFER unconditionally when the dispute has exactly one source per side (distinct-source-count==2)
fixProbes.note: Today a clean 1v1 RESOLVES=NOOP (breach=false). Blanket-defer would DEFER=true, BUT it is a tunable threshold: every legitimate binary correction (a true witness overturning a planted false canonical) now needs a human (hard-theorem tension as labor), it re-enables the DoS horn at linear cost, and it is trivially evaded by adding one self-stacked co-asserter (2v1, evaded=true). REJECT; prefer fp-1 (root-count) + fp-3 (domain-scoped margin).

fixProbes.fix: (e) order pendingBridges by earned_bridge_value; seedActivation = gamma * origin provenance_independence (soft, never hard-skip)
fixProbes.note: Today the sweep is identity-blind: poison surfaces=true at constant gamma. Soft gate: bare-key origin (indep 0) -> seed=0.000 < assembly floor (dropped=true); earned bridges fire first. PARTIAL: the sweep is deliberately MANDATORY/fail-open so a starved bridge still gets a degraded crossing or a BRIDGE_STARVED stamp; earned_bridge_value is offline-earnable so a patient attacker buys priority; and a HARD gate would starve genuine convergence=1 insight bridges and erode 'walk never witnesses'.

fixProbes.fix: Distinct independent-ROOT denominator (or exact per-source alpha-recompute) for defaultSurvivingMargin
fixProbes.note: Engine TODAY derives contributingStrandIds = winner + same-source members, so same-key echo pads are themselves tainted by disown(X) AND their equal rep flattens the decisive gap (the dispute DEFERRED). Either way there is NO locked fraudulent demotion (fraudLocked=false, reopened=false) — the same-key variant is ALREADY defended by the real engine. The residual is the proportional MODEL: margin*(N/(N+1))=0.300 >= 0.3 suppresses=true; a distinct-ROOT denominator => 0.165 re-opens=true. PARTIAL: distinct-root closes same-key; padding under DISTINCT cheap anchors degrades to the class-vs-root gap (exact alpha-recompute closes fully).

fixProbes.fix: One-line: pass taintedStrandIds (full demoted closure) instead of seedClawedBack to corroboration.eventsIntersecting
fixProbes.note: D is demoted by the sweep (demoted=true) but its event was NOT reversed today (B alpha unchanged=false, reversed=["corrob:0"]). Intersecting over the FULL closure {S,D} would reverse B=true at ~zero cost (idempotent via markReversed). CLOSES this hop; PARTIAL overall (does not close PT3 corroboration-of-corroboration); must still spare HARDENING-4 survived-demotion strands.

fixProbes.fix: Engine-derive corroboratingStrandIds from the web's computed agreement set (same-attribute LIVE agreers) + drop the no-name exemption
fixProbes.note: B named decoy not A: event recorded (reconcile balances earned==explained) yet disown(A) reversed=["corrob:0"] (B survived=false). Engine-derived agreement set names A=true => disown(A) reverses B=true. CLOSES misattribution + no-name (engine emits the event regardless of caller naming); a genuinely-independent ratify with no in-web agreer stays non-reversible by design (wall-with-a-window).
